title: Techno-economic assessment of renewable hydrogen production for mobility: A case study
short_title: Techno-economic assessment of renewable hydrogen production for mobility
journal_role_basis: Energy Conversion and Management is listed under applied_flagship in wiki/_meta/journal-role-vocab.md.
fulltext_source: main-pdf fulltext cache from Zotero storage; Zotero MCP metadata call was cancelled by tool layer.
peer_review_file_trap: Not applicable: Energy Conversion and Management is not a Nature Portfolio journal.
abstract: Renewable hydrogen produced by water electrolysis from intermittent energy sources is assessed through an integrated Sardinia case linking PV generation, a 5 MW PEM electrolyzer, compression/storage, and urban bus use. The paper reports 20.3 M EUR CAPEX, LCoH of 4.09 EUR/kg or 2.97 EUR/kg after oxygen and electricity revenues, and H2 bus STC of 1.45 EUR/km versus 1.04 EUR/km for diesel buses.
